phase_1.audio: Audio recording captures initial spike of 102.7 dBA, gradually settling to 72.7-83.7 dBA midway through session. Very loud environment detected; concentration significantly compromised.
phase_1.vitals: Heart rate averaged 88 bpm throughout session (consistently elevated, suggesting heightened stress or engagement). Data indicates user was in suboptimal physiological state for concentration.
phase_1.gps_motion: GPS signal strength inconsistent at start (3.5m error margin), achieving 2.4m lock after stabilization. Movement sensors indicate rapid transition to stillness; no significant repositioning detected.

phase_2.correlations: GPS stabilization timing aligns closely with motion state change. Audio context shows variation throughout captured period.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Session demonstrates strong focus-supportive conditions with low ambient noise (83.7 dB), minimal body repositioning.

phase_3.recommendations: Use active noise cancellation or white noise to counteract loud surroundings. Current 186s sessions reasonable; consider testing longer 30-min blocks.

reason: Score weighted toward audio environment (83.7dB—loud), stable positioning, and session duration (186s). Overall favorable conditions.
